spec_match.rationale: The gallery applies the exact border-area declaration when supported, but native rendering cannot be observed in this Headless Chrome 149 build; the unsupported path now uses and labels the two-layer fallback.

interactive.rationale: The native border-width slider and four pressed-state gradient controls update every gallery and comparison example in both native and fallback branches.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page demonstrates the promised gradient-border use case and explicitly separates native Chrome 150 behavior from the compatibility technique.

edge_cases.rationale: Rectangle, rounded, double, and dashed border examples, 4–40 px widths, four gradient types, and the unsupported-browser branch are all represented.

references.rationale: The page links ChromeStatus feature 6234471210811392 and the feature conformance route links the CSS Backgrounds Level 4 border-area section.

conformance.rationale: The route passes 3/8 assertions in Chrome 149; all five border-area-specific parser, CSSOM, layering, and observable-layout contracts fail because this browser predates the v150 feature.

runtime_evidence.rationale: DevTools MCP captured the live broken fallback, then verified the repaired local page at 16 px and 28 px with multiple gradients; content remains on the paper background, controls update, and console/network stay clean.

telemetry_health.rationale: The repaired interactions produced no runtime, resource, console, or assertion errors and the telemetry request returned HTTP 202.

design_system.rationale: The fallback uses shared paper and accent variables, preserves readable content contrast, and labels fallback output rather than presenting it as native border-area rendering.

accessibility.rationale: The support result and width output are live status text, the slider has a precise accessible name, and gradient choices expose native button names plus aria-pressed state.

openQuestions.title: Capture native border-area rendering in Chrome 150+
openQuestions.detail: Re-run the four gallery variants in a browser where all border-area conformance assertions pass and compare native dashed/double stroke clipping against the explicitly labelled two-layer fallback.

summary: The live page detected unsupported border-area but still applied the invalid declaration, so the gradient filled each content box instead of remaining in the border. Unsupported browsers now receive a labelled two-layer background fallback that keeps the content area clear. DevTools screenshots confirm the visual repair and the controls expose improved names and pressed/live state.
